meta.name: Olha Lys
meta.headline: Project Delivery • Transition • Transformation • Communication
meta.subtitle: A public, workbook-based Career OS built from documented project inventory, summaries, PMI detail, references and achievements.

projects.Portfolio / Engagement: Hydro AS – RFS Infrastructure Delivery Portfolio
projects.Scope / Portfolio: Managed a portfolio of ~40 customer-requested infrastructure projects (RFSs), including server builds, decommissions, database implementations, infrastructure changes, application deployments, and migration-related activities.

Assigned full-time to IBM's coordinated recovery response following the March 2019 Hydro cyberattack. As one of three IBM Project Managers, coordinated cross-functional recovery activities, prioritized restoration work, managed customer communication, and supported the phased recovery of critical IT infrastructure.
projects.Project Scale / Complexity: Enterprise-wide recovery effort involving multiple technical teams working 24/7 in coordination with the customer

projects.Scope / Portfolio: Managed multiple transition workstreams supporting the Carrefour "Move to India" transformation program, including VMware, Citrix, Windows Team Lead, Windows Health Check, Patching, Compliance Coordination and Governance activities. Responsible for project planning, stakeholder coordination, knowledge transfer, cutover planning, governance, RAID management and successful service transition.
projects.Project Scale / Complexity: Multi-workstream international transition program involving France, Czech Republic, Poland and India, contributing to the successful transition of 132 FTE with no client service impact.

projects.Portfolio / Engagement: Lufthansa FITO2 – MCMP AIOps Reporting Migration
projects.Scope / Portfolio: Led the migration of enterprise operational reporting from HP OBR to the MCMP AIOps reporting platform as part of the Lufthansa FITO2 transformation program. Responsible for project governance, scope definition, executive reporting, stakeholder coordination, dependency management, solution planning, migration oversight and business readiness across multiple technical teams.
projects.Domain: Enterprise Transformation • AIOps • IT Service Management
projects.Project Scale / Complexity: Enterprise reporting migration within the Lufthansa FITO2 transformation program involving architects, AIOps specialists, account teams, BAU representatives and multiple dependent projects across the System Management Tooling tower.

projects.Scope / Portfolio: Coordinated the transition and adaptation of Lufthansa FITO I Billing & Consumption processes to the FITO II operating model. Managed invoicing, consumption metering, ServiceNow integration, stakeholder alignment, business process coordination and governance across commercial, financial and technical teams.
projects.Domain: Business Process Transformation • IT Service Management • ServiceNow • Commercial Operations
projects.Project Scale / Complexity: Cross-functional transformation project involving Lufthansa business representatives, commercial teams, finance, architects, ServiceNow specialists and multiple FITO2 workstreams supporting implementation of new Billing & Consumption processes.

projects.Scope / Portfolio: Led communication governance for the Lighthouse 2 enterprise transformation program, coordinating internal and external communication strategies, stakeholder engagement, migration communication, change calendars, Go-Live communication, hypercare communication and executive reporting across multiple transformation workstreams supporting business transformation, AWS migration and technology modernization.
projects.Domain: Enterprise Transformation • Change Management • Executive Communication
projects.Project Scale / Complexity: Enterprise-wide transformation communication supporting multiple business and technology workstreams, customer communication, migration events and change governance across Luminor, IBM and Kyndryl organizations.

summary.Business Objective: Deliver infrastructure requests for Hydro AS
summary.Responsibilities: Planned, coordinated, executed, monitored and closed infrastructure projects

summary.Business Objective: Coordinate IBM's infrastructure recovery activities following the Hydro cyberattack to support the phased restoration of critical business services.
summary.Scope: Enterprise-wide cyber recovery involving multiple cross-functional recovery teams operating 24/7.
summary.Responsibilities: Coordinated recovery activities, prioritized restoration work, managed stakeholder communication, aligned technical teams, tracked recovery progress and supported executive reporting during the crisis response.
summary.Deliverables: Recovery coordination, restoration planning, stakeholder communication, recovery tracking, status reporting.
summary.Achievements: Served as one of three IBM Project Managers dedicated full-time to coordinating IBM's recovery response during one of the largest publicly reported ransomware incidents affecting an industrial enterprise.
summary.Relevance: Crisis Management • Stakeholder Management • Business Continuity • Leadership Under Pressure

summary.Business Objective: Successfully transition multiple IT infrastructure services from European delivery centers to India while ensuring knowledge transfer, operational readiness, governance compliance, and uninterrupted customer service.
summary.Scope: Managed multiple transition workstreams including VMware, Citrix, Windows Team Lead, Windows Health Check, Patching, Compliance Coordination and governance activities as part of the Carrefour ARGO "Move to India" transformation program.
summary.Responsibilities: Planned and coordinated transition projects; organized project kick-offs; developed Project Definition Reports (PDRs); managed stakeholders, RAID logs, schedules, knowledge transfer, shadow mode, cutover, governance, risks, issues, dependencies, project reporting and customer communication across international teams.
summary.Deliverables: Project Definition Reports (PDRs), transition plans, KT plans, governance documentation, RAID logs, meeting minutes, stakeholder communication, project schedules, cutover plans, acceptance documentation, project closure artifacts.
summary.Achievements: Successfully contributed to the Carrefour ARGO transformation program by coordinating multiple transition workstreams that supported the successful transfer of 132 FTE to India with no client service disruption. The project team achieved key contractual milestones despite COVID-19 constraints and received formal recognition from IBM Transition & Transformation leadership.
summary.Relevance: Transition Management • Program Governance • Stakeholder Management • Knowledge Transfer • International Delivery • Risk Management • Change Management • Project Governance

summary.Business Objective: Replace the legacy HP OBR reporting solution with the MCMP AIOps Reporting platform to modernize operational reporting, improve reporting capabilities, and support Lufthansa's FITO2 transformation program.
summary.Scope: Managed the end-to-end reporting migration project, including solution planning, scope definition, stakeholder alignment, architecture coordination, dependency management, report migration planning, testing, deployment, education and business readiness.
summary.Responsibilities: Developed and maintained the Project Definition Report (PDR); defined project scope, milestones and acceptance criteria; coordinated architects, SMEs and AIOps teams; managed risks, dependencies and project reporting; prepared executive status reports, chaired workshops, coordinated customer communication and supported project governance throughout the project lifecycle.
summary.Deliverables: Project Definition Report (PDR), project plans, executive status reports, governance documentation, migration roadmap, milestone tracking, risk management, stakeholder workshops, migration planning, education plan and Go-Live readiness.
summary.Achievements: Successfully established governance for the MCMP AIOps Reporting Migration, coordinated multiple technical and business stakeholders, and delivered executive-level reporting throughout the transformation. Recognized as the Project Manager for Workstream 6.7 within the Lufthansa FITO2 Transformation Program.
summary.Relevance: Enterprise Transformation • Executive Reporting • Governance • Stakeholder Management • AIOps • Risk Management • Project Leadership

summary.Business Objective: Establish and transition the Billing & Consumption processes for the Lufthansa FITO2 operating model, enabling accurate service consumption tracking, invoicing, and ServiceNow-based commercial processes.
summary.Scope: Coordinated the transition and implementation of Billing & Consumption processes, ServiceNow integration, invoicing, consumption reporting, complaint management and stakeholder alignment across commercial, finance and technical organizations.
summary.Responsibilities: Coordinated project planning, stakeholder management, customer workshops, business process alignment, ServiceNow integration activities, consumption evidence definition, invoicing process implementation, project reporting, governance meetings, release coordination and handover planning.
summary.Deliverables: Billing & Consumption governance, project reporting, process documentation, meeting minutes, consumption evidence definition, ServiceNow integration support, invoicing process implementation, customer workshops, handover documentation.
summary.Achievements: Successfully took over the Billing & Consumption project after the Reporting Migration workstream stabilized, maintaining project continuity while coordinating commercial, financial and technical stakeholders across the Lufthansa FITO2 program.
summary.Relevance: Business Transformation • ServiceNow • Commercial Processes • Stakeholder Management • Governance • Service Integration • Change Management

summary.Business Objective: Establish and govern communication across the Lighthouse 2 enterprise transformation program, ensuring timely, accurate and coordinated communication for customers, business stakeholders, project teams and executive leadership throughout migration, release and Go-Live activities.
summary.Scope: Managed communication governance for multiple transformation workstreams covering business transformation, AWS migration, Journey to Cloud, infrastructure modernization, major releases, migration events, hypercare, customer notifications and executive communication across Kyndryl, IBM and Luminor organizations.
summary.Responsibilities: Developed communication strategies, governance processes and communication plans; coordinated stakeholders across business and technology teams; prepared Go-Live notifications, migration communication, customer messaging and executive updates; maintained release calendars, change communication, migration event coordination, hypercare communication and communication approval processes; aligned messaging across Luminor, IBM and Kyndryl organizations.
summary.Deliverables: Enterprise Communication Plan, migration communication templates, Go-Live announcements, communication governance framework, release communication calendar, stakeholder communication matrix, migration event communication plans, hypercare communication plans, executive communication materials, change calendars and communication procedures.
summary.Achievements: Established a structured communication governance framework supporting the Lighthouse 2 transformation program, standardizing communication across multiple project workstreams, migration waves, customer-facing activities and major releases while improving stakeholder alignment in a regulated banking environment.
summary.Relevance: Enterprise Transformation • Change Management • Executive Communication • Stakeholder Management • Communication Governance • Release Management • Go-Live Readiness • Hypercare • Banking Transformation

pmi.Project Name: Hydro AS – RFS Infrastructure Delivery Portfolio
pmi.Project Objective: Deliver customer-requested infrastructure services through a portfolio of Request for Service (RFS) projects while meeting agreed scope, schedule and quality objectives.
pmi.My Role: Managed a portfolio of approximately 40 infrastructure projects, leading project planning, stakeholder coordination, customer communication, technical team alignment and project governance from initiation through successful closure.
pmi.Responsibilities: Defined project scope, developed delivery plans, coordinated architects and infrastructure teams, managed project schedules, risks, issues and dependencies, maintained customer communication, prepared project documentation (SoW, DOU, PDR), monitored progress and ensured successful project completion.
pmi.Outcome: Successfully delivered a portfolio of approximately 40 infrastructure projects, maintaining delivery quality, governance compliance and customer satisfaction while establishing my first formal Project Manager experience.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

pmi.Project Objective: Support the coordinated recovery of Hydro's critical IT infrastructure following the March 2019 cyberattack by restoring IT services, coordinating recovery activities, and enabling business continuity.
pmi.My Role: Assigned full-time as one of three IBM Project Managers dedicated to coordinating the infrastructure recovery effort. Led coordination across cross-functional technical teams, prioritized restoration activities, managed stakeholder communication with on-site customer representatives, tracked recovery progress, and supported decision-making throughout the recovery lifecycle.
pmi.Responsibilities: Coordinated recovery work across multiple technical teams; facilitated prioritization of restoration activities; maintained continuous communication with customer representatives; tracked recovery status, risks and dependencies; coordinated recovery planning meetings; supported resource alignment; monitored progress toward restoration milestones during 24/7 recovery operations.
pmi.Outcome: Contributed to the structured recovery of critical IT infrastructure following one of the most significant ransomware incidents affecting an industrial enterprise, enabling the phased restoration of business services through coordinated project management and stakeholder communication.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

pmi.Project Objective: Lead multiple infrastructure service transition workstreams within Carrefour's ARGO "Move to India" transformation program, ensuring successful knowledge transfer, governance, stakeholder alignment and seamless service transition from European delivery centers to India without business disruption.
pmi.My Role: Managed multiple transition workstreams within the Carrefour ARGO Transformation Program, leading project planning, governance, stakeholder coordination, project documentation, knowledge transfer, cutover preparation and project reporting. Developed Project Definition Reports (PDRs), facilitated kick-off meetings, coordinated international delivery teams and ensured contractual milestones were successfully achieved.
pmi.Responsibilities: Defined project scope and objectives; developed transition schedules and work plans; coordinated sending and receiving teams across Czech Republic, France and India; managed stakeholders, risks, issues and dependencies; maintained RAID logs; organized governance meetings and project reporting; coordinated knowledge transfer, shadow mode, parallel run, cutover and aftercare activities; prepared and maintained PDRs, meeting minutes and project documentation; monitored contractual milestones and supported project closure.
pmi.Outcome: Successfully coordinated multiple transition workstreams contributing to Carrefour's international "Move to India" transformation program. Supported the successful transfer of infrastructure services while maintaining service continuity and governance throughout the transition lifecycle. The project team achieved key contractual milestones despite COVID-19 constraints and was formally recognized by IBM Transition & Transformation EMEA leadership for delivery excellence.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

pmi.Project Name: Lufthansa FITO2 – MCMP AIOps Reporting Migration
pmi.Project Objective: Lead the migration of enterprise operational reporting from the legacy HP OBR platform to the MCMP AIOps Reporting solution as part of the Lufthansa FITO2 transformation program, enabling modernized reporting, governance and operational visibility.
pmi.My Role: Led the reporting migration project from initiation through deployment, developing the Project Definition Report (PDR), defining scope, milestones, governance, risks and dependencies, coordinating architects, SMEs and AIOps teams, preparing executive reporting and driving stakeholder alignment. Acted as deputy for the Tower Lead Project Manager during absences, ensuring continuity of project governance and customer communication.
pmi.Responsibilities: Defined project scope, objectives and acceptance criteria; managed project planning, executive reporting, risks, issues and dependencies; coordinated workshops, architecture reviews and customer meetings; maintained project documentation, RAID, project schedules and governance; monitored milestones, supported deployment readiness, training and Go-Live activities.
pmi.Outcome: Successfully delivered the governance and coordination of the MCMP AIOps Reporting Migration within the Lufthansa FITO2 transformation program, providing executive reporting, managing cross-project dependencies and supporting successful business readiness and deployment of the new reporting solution.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

pmi.Project Objective: Coordinate the implementation of Billing & Consumption processes supporting the Lufthansa FITO2 operating model, enabling accurate service consumption tracking, invoicing and ServiceNow-based commercial processes.
pmi.My Role: Took over the Billing & Consumption project following completion of the main Reporting Migration workstream, coordinating commercial, financial and technical stakeholders, supporting ServiceNow integration, customer governance, invoicing processes, consumption reporting and business readiness.
pmi.Responsibilities: Coordinated project planning, stakeholder communication, governance meetings, customer workshops, business process alignment, ServiceNow integration, consumption evidence definition, invoicing implementation, release planning, project reporting and handover activities across multiple teams.
pmi.Outcome: Successfully maintained project continuity after taking over the Billing & Consumption workstream, supporting implementation of new invoicing and consumption processes, coordinating cross-functional stakeholders and ensuring alignment between Lufthansa and Kyndryl teams.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

pmi.Project Name: Luminor Lighthouse 2 – Transformation Communication & Change Management
pmi.Project Objective: Establish and govern communication across the Lighthouse 2 enterprise transformation program, ensuring timely, accurate and coordinated communication for customers, business stakeholders, project teams and executive leadership throughout migration, release and Go-Live activities supporting Luminor's technology transformation.
pmi.My Role: Led the Communication Management workstream within the Lighthouse 2 transformation program, developing communication governance, stakeholder communication strategies, migration communication templates, Go-Live communication, executive messaging, customer communication and release communication processes. Coordinated communication between Luminor, IBM and Kyndryl organizations while supporting organizational readiness and business change activities.
pmi.Responsibilities: Developed enterprise communication plans; defined communication governance principles; coordinated stakeholder communication across business and technical organizations; prepared customer notifications, migration communications, Go-Live announcements and executive updates; maintained release calendars and change communication schedules; coordinated hypercare communication, migration event communication, communication approvals and communication readiness for transformation releases.
pmi.Outcome: Successfully established a structured communication governance framework supporting the Lighthouse 2 enterprise transformation program, enabling consistent communication across multiple transformation workstreams, migration events, customer-facing activities and major releases while improving stakeholder coordination within a regulated banking environment.
pmi.PMI Process Groups: Initiating • Planning • Executing • Monitoring & Controlling • Closing

references.Position: Systems Management Tooling Tower Lead / Senior Project Manager

references.Position: Transformation Program Lead / Service Management Integration Lead

Coordinated business, finance and technical stakeholders, customer governance, invoicing processes and ServiceNow integration. Customer requested continuity of project leadership when reassignment was planned, demonstrating stakeholder trust.

references.Notes: Led the Communication Management workstream for the Lighthouse 2 transformation program. Developed communication governance, migration communication strategy, Go-Live communication, release calendars, stakeholder communication plans and customer communication processes supporting multiple transformation workstreams across IBM, Kyndryl and Luminor. Member of the official Lighthouse 2 Communication Coordination Team together with Luminor Communication and PMO leadership.
